title: RNA Isolation from Human Intervertebral Disc Tissue
author: Nashrah Ahmad

steps.title: Tissue collection and storage
steps.instruction: Tissue collection and storage

steps.title: Human lower thoracic and entire lumbar...
steps.instruction: Human lower thoracic and entire lumbar spines obtained from an organ procurement organization should be processed within 48 hours post-mortem.

steps.title: After resecting IVDs from the spine,...
steps.instruction: After resecting IVDs from the spine, separate AF and NP, cut into smaller pieces and immediately place them in 4 ml of Allprotect® in 5 ml Eppendorf tubes and store at 4°C overnight, then place into -80°C for long-term storage.

steps.title: Removal of Allprotect®, weighing, and tissue preparation
steps.instruction: Removal of Allprotect®, weighing, and tissue preparation

steps.title: Thaw the tissue
steps.instruction: Thaw the tissue.

steps.title: Gently remove residual Allprotect® using a...
steps.instruction: Gently remove residual Allprotect® using a Kimwipe and briefly rinse the tissue with PBS.

steps.title: Cut off a section from the...
steps.instruction: Cut off a section from the tissue of approximately target amount. For AF, use ~50 mg; for NP, ~200 mg and weigh. Cut additional slice as necessary.

steps.title: Snap-freeze the tissue in liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Snap-freeze the tissue in liquid nitrogen and transfer it to a new tube on dry ice (or keep in LN₂) until further processing. NOTE: Scale up subsequent reagent volumes proportionally for NP. The same pulverization and homogenization procedure applies to both AF and NP. Lysate from NP tissue was divided across four columns after the ethanol precipitation step.

steps.title: Mechanical pulverization
steps.instruction: Mechanical pulverization

steps.title: Pre-cool the tissue pulverizer (cell crusher) with LN₂
steps.instruction: Pre-cool the tissue pulverizer (cell crusher) with LN₂.

steps.title: Place the frozen tissue into the...
steps.instruction: Place the frozen tissue into the pre-cooled cell crusher, add LN₂, and pulverize to a fine powder using a hammer.

steps.title: Rotate the cell crusher after each impact for thorough pulverization
steps.instruction: Rotate the cell crusher after each impact for thorough pulverization.

steps.title: Continue until a fine powder is achieved
steps.instruction: Continue until a fine powder is achieved. This procedure should be done quickly so that the cell crusher and tissue do not warm up.

steps.title: Homogenization in TRIzol™
steps.instruction: Homogenization in TRIzol™

steps.title: Transfer the pulverized tissue to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the pulverized tissue to a 2 mL tube containing 1 mL of ice-cold TRIzol™ using a clean spatula.

steps.title: Vortex for 20 seconds, then place...
steps.instruction: Vortex for 20 seconds, then place on ice for 1 minute. Repeat 2 more times.

steps.title: Incubate the samples on a rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples on a rotator at 4°C for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (~1 mL) to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (~1 mL) to a new pre-cooled 2 mL tube and store at −80°C or proceed to the next step.

steps.title: First extraction
steps.instruction: First extraction

steps.title: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the homogenate in TRIzol™
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the homogenate in TRIzol™.

steps.title: Shake vigorously for 15 seconds and...
steps.instruction: Shake vigorously for 15 seconds and incubate for 2 minutes at room temperature (RT).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully collect the upper aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Carefully collect the upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Second extraction
steps.instruction: Second extraction

steps.title: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase.

steps.title: Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at...
steps.instruction: Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at RT, and centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Collect the upper aqueous phase and...
steps.instruction: Collect the upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Third extraction
steps.instruction: Third extraction

steps.title: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase.

steps.title: Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at...
steps.instruction: Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at RT, and centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Collect the final upper aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Collect the final upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Ethanol precipitation
steps.instruction: Ethanol precipitation

steps.title: Add an equal volume (1:1) of 70% RNase-free ethanol (e
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume (1:1) of 70% RNase-free ethanol (e.g., 300–400 μL) to the aqueous phase.

steps.title: Mix gently and incubate on ice for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Mix gently and incubate on ice for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Binding to the RNeasy column
steps.instruction: Binding to the RNeasy column

steps.title: Load 700 μL of the sample...
steps.instruction: Load 700 μL of the sample onto an RNeasy spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Repeat until the entire sample has passed through the column
steps.instruction: Repeat until the entire sample has passed through the column.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: For NP samples divide the sample into four parts and load onto four RNeasy spin columns to prevent clogging.

steps.title: First wash
steps.instruction: First wash

steps.title: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.

steps.title: DNase treatment (first round)
steps.instruction: DNase treatment (first round)

steps.title: Prepare the DNase reaction by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare the DNase reaction by adding 70 μL of DNA digestion buffer (Zymo) to 10 μL of Zymo DNase I (total 80 μL).

steps.title: Pipette the entire 80 μL directly onto the column membrane
steps.instruction: Pipette the entire 80 μL directly onto the column membrane.

steps.title: Incubate for 15 minutes at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 15 minutes at RT.

steps.title: Second wash
steps.instruction: Second wash

steps.title: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.

steps.title: DNase treatment (second round)
steps.instruction: DNase treatment (second round)

steps.title: Prepare another DNase reaction
steps.instruction: Prepare another DNase reaction: add 70 μL of DNA digestion buffer (Zymo) to 10 μL of Zymo DNase I (80 μL total).

steps.title: Pipette the 80 μL directly onto the column membrane
steps.instruction: Pipette the 80 μL directly onto the column membrane.

steps.title: Incubate for 15 minutes at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 15 minutes at RT.

steps.title: Third wash
steps.instruction: Third wash

steps.title: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.

steps.title: Buffer RPE washes
steps.instruction: Buffer RPE washes

steps.title: Transfer the spin column to a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a new collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of Buffer RPE
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of Buffer RPE.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add another 500 μL of Buffer RPE
steps.instruction: Add another 500 μL of Buffer RPE.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at >8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Final drying spin
steps.instruction: Final drying spin

steps.title: Place the RNeasy column in a...
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy column in a new 2 mL collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at full...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at full speed (16,000 × g) at RT to dry the membrane thoroughly.

steps.title: Elution and storage
steps.instruction: Elution and storage

steps.title: Transfer the RNeasy column to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the RNeasy column to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 15 μL of RNase-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 15 μL of RNase-free water to the membrane and incubate for 1 minute.

steps.title: Centrifuge at >8,000 rpm for 15 seconds at RT
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at >8,000 rpm for 15 seconds at RT.

steps.title: Return the same 15 μL eluate...
steps.instruction: Return the same 15 μL eluate onto the column membrane, incubate for 1 minute, and centrifuge again for 1 minute at >8,000 rpm at RT.

steps.title: Store the eluted RNA at −80°C until use
steps.instruction: Store the eluted RNA at −80°C until use.

steps.title: Spectrophotometric analysis
steps.instruction: Spectrophotometric analysis

steps.title: Apply 1 μL of RNA to...
steps.instruction: Apply 1 μL of RNA to a NanoDrop™ 1000 (NanoDrop Technologies) to measure RNA concentration and purity.

steps.title: Integrity analysis
steps.instruction: Integrity analysis

steps.title: Use 2 μL of RNA on...
steps.instruction: Use 2 μL of RNA on the 4150 TapeStation System (Agilent Technologies) to determine RNA Integrity Number (RIN).
